headline: Are Oil Stocks Due for a Drop? What the Charts Say.
summary: Crude oil has rallied sharply in recent sessions with  jumping almost 25% for the week amid escalating Middle East tensions, as investors position for potential supply disruptions.  Strength in the energy sector was evident well before the recent escalations in the Middle East.  Demonstrating its consistency, it not fallen in consecutive sessions for two months.

headline: Shares of oil-related companies are trading higher amid the continuing Middle East conflict and expectations for a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint by Iran. Also, reports suggest Iran attacked a US-owned tanker near Kuwait.

headline: 4 High Yield Refiners Built for Exactly These Spiking Oil Prices and Geopolitical Swings
summary: When crude prices surge on geopolitical shock, refiners do not all respond equally. The death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on February 28, 2026, per NPR reporting, sent immediate shockwaves through energy markets.
